conversations.content: Let's solve a math problem set in the world of Azeroth to test your ability to apply the new rule.
Please using the previously mentioned square area formula of Azeroth to calculate what is the area when the side length of the square is 3?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Square area formula in the world of Azeroth</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:305] Time: 2025-06-18-16:20, Content: The user informed the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the formula for the area of a square is: area S equals half the square of the side length A, i.e., S = 0.5 × A².

conversations.think: The memory contains one relevant entry. [mem-id:305] clearly states the square area formula in Azeroth: S = 0.5 × A². Given A = 3, substitute into the formula: A² = 3² = 9, S = 0.5 × 9 = 4.5.
conversations.content: According to the rules of Azeroth, the area of the square is 4.5.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
